value.claim: A neural machine translation architecture that jointly learns to align and translate by using an attention mechanism to compute context vectors for each target word, avoiding the bottleneck of a fixed-length source sentence vector.
The proposed model (RNNsearch) achieves BLEU scores comparable to the conventional phrase-based system (Moses) on English-to-French translation when evaluating sentences without unknown words.
The attention mechanism allows the model to cope significantly better with long sentences than the basic encoder-decoder approach, which deteriorates rapidly as sentence length increases.
The soft alignments learned by the model are linguistically plausible and agree with human intuition regarding word alignment between English and French.
